title: Type III AC joint separation: nonoperative vs operative
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute Grade III acromioclavicular joint separation, is initial nonoperative management or operative stabilization preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Most evidence supports initial nonoperative care with similar medium-term function; high-demand overhead and heavy-labor patients remain debated.

title: Displaced midshaft clavicle fracture: nonoperative vs ORIF
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced midshaft clavicle fracture, is nonoperative management or open reduction and internal fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgery lowers nonunion and symptomatic malunion but adds operative risk; benefit concentrates with greater shortening or displacement, and thresholds are debated.

title: 3-/4-part proximal humerus fracture (older adult): nonop vs surgery
canonical_question: In an older adult with a displaced 3- or 4-part proximal humerus fracture, is nonoperative management or surgery (fixation or arthroplasty) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: PROFHER showed no clear benefit of surgery over nonoperative care for many; patient selection and fixation-versus-arthroplasty remain contested.

title: Degenerative rotator cuff tear (older patient): repair vs physical therapy
canonical_question: In an older adult with a symptomatic degenerative atraumatic rotator cuff tear, is structured physical therapy or surgical repair preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Several trials show comparable patient-reported outcomes at medium term; tear progression and age and activity modify the choice.

title: First-time anterior shoulder dislocation (young athlete): nonop vs early stabilization
canonical_question: In a young athlete after a first-time traumatic anterior shoulder dislocation, is nonoperative management or early surgical stabilization preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Very high recurrence in young contact athletes argues for early stabilization, yet many first dislocations are still managed nonoperatively.

title: ACL rupture: early reconstruction vs structured rehab
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute ACL rupture, is early reconstruction or structured rehabilitation with optional delayed reconstruction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: KANON showed rehabilitation with optional delayed reconstruction non-inferior for many patients; activity demands and instability drive the decision.

title: ACL reconstruction graft choice: BPTB vs hamstring vs quadriceps autograft
canonical_question: When ACL reconstruction is chosen, is a bone-patellar tendon-bone, hamstring, or quadriceps tendon autograft preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Graft choices trade donor-site morbidity, re-rupture rates, and residual laxity differently; no single graft dominates across patients.

title: First-time patellar dislocation: nonop vs MPFL reconstruction
canonical_question: After a first-time lateral patellar dislocation, is nonoperative management or MPFL reconstruction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Recurrence risk with osteochondral injury or anatomic risk factors argues for surgery, while many first dislocations are managed nonoperatively.

title: Unicompartmental knee OA (younger, malaligned): HTO vs UKA
canonical_question: In a younger patient with isolated unicompartmental knee OA and malalignment, is high tibial osteotomy or unicompartmental arthroplasty preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Both are defensible; the choice depends on age, activity, alignment, and surgeon experience, with genuine practice variation.

title: Displaced femoral neck fracture (active older adult): THA vs hemiarthroplasty
canonical_question: In an active independent older adult with a displaced femoral neck fracture, is total hip arthroplasty or hemiarthroplasty preferred?
equipoise_rationale: HEALTH showed modest rather than decisive differences; THA may benefit higher-function patients but carries higher dislocation risk.

title: Femoroacetabular impingement: arthroscopy vs physical therapy
canonical_question: In an adult with symptomatic femoroacetabular impingement, is arthroscopic surgery or physiotherapy-led management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: FASHIoN favored arthroscopy on average, but the effect size and appropriate patient selection remain debated.

title: Lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy (no progressive deficit): microdiscectomy vs conservative
canonical_question: In an adult with lumbar disc herniation and radiculopathy but no progressive neurologic deficit, is conservative management or microdiscectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: SPORT showed both improve, with faster early relief from surgery; high crossover makes the long-term difference contested.

title: Degenerative spondylolisthesis: decompression alone vs decompression + fusion
canonical_question: In an adult with symptomatic single-level degenerative spondylolisthesis requiring surgery, is decompression alone or decompression with fusion preferred?
equipoise_rationale: SLIP and the Swedish trial reached opposing conclusions, making this a classic unresolved controversy.

title: Acute Achilles tendon rupture: operative vs functional nonoperative
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute Achilles tendon rupture, is operative repair or functional nonoperative rehabilitation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Modern functional rehabilitation narrows the re-rupture gap; surgery trades a small re-rupture reduction against operative risks.

title: Isolated stable lateral malleolus (Weber B) fracture: nonop vs ORIF
canonical_question: In an adult with an isolated minimally displaced stable Weber B lateral malleolus fracture, is nonoperative management or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Stable patterns often do well nonoperatively; stability assessment and displacement thresholds drive variation.

title: Displaced intra-articular calcaneus fracture: ORIF vs nonoperative
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced intra-articular calcaneal fracture, is operative fixation or nonoperative management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The UK Heel Fracture trial found little overall benefit to surgery with added wound risk; patient selection remains contested.

title: Distal radius fracture (lower-demand older adult): cast vs ORIF
canonical_question: In a lower-demand older adult with a displaced distal radius fracture and acceptable reduction, is cast immobilization or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Function is often similar in low-demand elderly despite radiographic differences; surgery is debated by demand and deformity.

title: Closed tibial shaft fracture: cast vs intramedullary nail
canonical_question: In an adult with a closed tibial shaft fracture, is cast or functional bracing, or intramedullary nailing preferred?
equipoise_rationale: A historic controversy; nailing is favored for displaced or unstable patterns, but acceptable-alignment fractures show practice variation.

title: Pediatric supracondylar humerus fracture (Gartland II): casting vs pinning
canonical_question: In a child with a Gartland type II supracondylar humerus fracture, is closed reduction with casting or closed reduction with percutaneous pinning preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Type II management varies by degree of displacement and by surgeon; both casting and pinning are in use.

title: Distal biceps tendon rupture: operative vs nonoperative
canonical_question: In an adult with a complete distal biceps tendon rupture, is operative repair or nonoperative management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgery restores supination and flexion strength; lower-demand patients may accept the nonoperative strength deficits.

title: Symptomatic hallux valgus: surgery vs conservative
canonical_question: In an adult with symptomatic hallux valgus, is conservative management or corrective surgery preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgery improves symptomatic deformity, but indications and timing vary and conservative care has limited effect on deformity.

title: Massive irreparable rotator cuff tear: joint-preserving vs reverse arthroplasty
canonical_question: In a patient with a massive irreparable rotator cuff tear, is a joint-preserving reconstruction (debridement/capsular/transfer) or reverse shoulder arthroplasty preferred?
equipoise_rationale: A rapidly evolving area; capsular reconstruction, transfers, balloon spacers, and reverse arthroplasty compete without a settled hierarchy.

title: Osteoporotic vertebral compression fracture: augmentation vs conservative
canonical_question: In an older adult with a painful osteoporotic vertebral compression fracture, is conservative management or vertebral augmentation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Sham-controlled trials were negative while later trials and registries dispute this; the area remains actively contested.

title: Open fracture: strict 6-hour vs urgent debridement timing
canonical_question: In an open long-bone fracture, must surgical debridement occur within 6 hours, or is urgent but not strictly sub-6-hour debridement with early antibiotics acceptable?
equipoise_rationale: The strict 6-hour rule is increasingly challenged, with emphasis shifting to early antibiotic administration; guidance is in flux.

title: Degenerative meniscal tear (no mechanical locking): APM vs physical therapy
canonical_question: In a middle-aged adult with a degenerative meniscal tear and no true mechanical locking, is physical therapy or arthroscopic partial meniscectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Multiple randomized trials show no benefit of arthroscopic partial meniscectomy over physical therapy absent true mechanical symptoms.

title: Subacromial impingement: decompression surgery vs conservative
canonical_question: In an adult with subacromial pain and an intact cuff, is conservative management or arthroscopic subacromial decompression preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Sham-controlled trials such as CSAW show no meaningful benefit of decompression over conservative or placebo treatment.

title: Acute uncomplicated lateral ankle sprain: functional rehab vs surgery
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute uncomplicated lateral ankle sprain, is functional rehabilitation or acute surgical repair preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Functional rehabilitation is standard; acute surgery is not indicated for uncomplicated sprains.

title: Acute uncomplicated mechanical low back pain: conservative management
canonical_question: In an adult with acute uncomplicated mechanical low back pain and no red flags, is conservative management or surgical evaluation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Guidelines uniformly support conservative care; surgery is not indicated without specific structural pathology.

title: Acute septic native joint: urgent surgical drainage
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute septic native joint, is urgent surgical drainage or antibiotics-alone management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Urgent surgical drainage plus antibiotics is standard of care; delay risks rapid joint destruction.

title: Cauda equina syndrome: emergent decompression
canonical_question: In an adult with acute cauda equina syndrome, is emergent surgical decompression or non-urgent management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Emergent decompression is standard; delay risks permanent bladder, bowel, and neurologic injury.

title: Acute compartment syndrome: emergent fasciotomy
canonical_question: In a limb with acute compartment syndrome, is emergent fasciotomy or observation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Emergent fasciotomy is standard; delay causes irreversible muscle and nerve necrosis.

title: Complete quadriceps tendon rupture (active adult): operative repair
canonical_question: In an active adult with a complete quadriceps tendon rupture and an extensor lag, is operative repair or nonoperative management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Operative repair is standard to restore the extensor mechanism in complete ruptures.

title: Hip fracture (medically fit older adult): early surgery vs delay
canonical_question: In a medically fit older adult with a hip fracture, is early surgery within 24 to 48 hours or deliberate delay preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Early surgery when the patient is medically fit is supported by guidelines and outcome data; avoidable delay worsens outcomes.

title: Displaced femoral neck fracture (young adult): urgent fixation
canonical_question: In a young adult with a displaced femoral neck fracture, is urgent reduction and fixation or nonoperative management preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Head-preserving urgent fixation is standard in young adults; nonoperative care risks nonunion and osteonecrosis. Timing and approach are still debated.

title: Closed displaced humeral shaft fracture: plate osteosynthesis vs functional bracing
canonical_question: In an adult with a closed displaced humeral shaft fracture, is plate osteosynthesis or functional bracing preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The FISH trial demonstrated comparable 5-year DASH scores, but 32% of bracing patients required secondary surgery for nonunion, making initial surgery highly cost-effective from a societal perspective.

title: Unstable ankle fracture in patients >60 years: close contact casting vs ORIF
canonical_question: In an older adult (age >60) with an unstable ankle fracture, is close contact casting or open reduction internal fixation (ORIF) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The AIM trial demonstrated clinical equivalence in ankle function (OMAS) at 6 months; ORIF has higher wound risks, whereas casting has higher malunion rates but is highly cost-effective.

title: Medial compartment knee osteoarthritis: partial vs total knee replacement
canonical_question: In an adult with isolated medial compartment knee osteoarthritis, is unicompartmental (partial) knee arthroplasty or total knee arthroplasty preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The TOPKAT trial showed similar 10-year clinical outcomes, reoperations, and revisions, but partial knee replacement had fewer complications and was more cost-effective.

title: Displaced olecranon fracture in patients >=75 years: nonoperative vs surgery
canonical_question: In an older adult (age >=75) with a displaced isolated olecranon fracture, is nonoperative management or surgical fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Randomized data show comparable DASH scores at 1 year; surgical groups have extremely high complication rates (up to 80%), and nonunions in the nonoperative group are mostly asymptomatic and well-tolerated.

title: Undisplaced scaphoid waist fracture: cast immobilization vs surgical fixation
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute scan-confirmed undisplaced scaphoid waist fracture, is cast immobilization or percutaneous screw fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The SWIFT trial showed no difference in long-term functional recovery or nonunion rates between groups, making initial cast immobilization highly cost-effective; surgery provides faster early mobilization but adds surgical risks.

title: Primary frozen shoulder in secondary care: structured physical therapy vs surgical intervention
canonical_question: In an adult with primary frozen shoulder (adhesive capsulitis), is early structured physical therapy or surgical intervention (MUA or arthroscopic capsular release) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The UK FROST trial found no superior patient-reported outcomes for pain and function at 12 months between early structured physical therapy, manipulation under anesthesia (MUA), or arthroscopic capsular release.

title: Lumbar spinal stenosis: decompression surgery vs physical therapy
canonical_question: In an adult with lumbar spinal stenosis without spondylolisthesis, is surgical decompression or structured physical therapy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Both modalities yield significant symptom improvement; surgery offers faster, more pronounced relief, but carries typical operative risks and high crossover from physical therapy.

title: Single-level cervical radiculopathy: ACDF vs structured physical therapy
canonical_question: In an adult with single-level cervical radiculopathy without progressive deficits, is anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) or physical therapy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Long-term functional outcomes converge, but surgery provides faster early pain relief and recovery at the cost of surgical exposure risks and adjacent segment disease.

title: Pediatric trigger thumb: surgical release vs observation and stretching
canonical_question: In a pediatric patient (age <3) with a trigger thumb, is surgical release or observation with stretching preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Spontaneous resolution is common in children under 2, but surgical release provides a definitive, low-risk cure for persistent or late-presenting triggering.

title: Subtrochanteric femur fracture: intramedullary nail vs plate fixation
canonical_question: In an adult with a subtrochanteric femur fracture, is intramedullary nailing or open reduction with plate fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Nailing offers biomechanical advantages and less dissection but is technically demanding; plating offers direct anatomical reduction but increases blood loss and infection risk.

title: Displaced patellar fracture with intact extensor: ORIF vs nonoperative
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced patellar fracture (<2mm step-off) and an intact extensor mechanism, is nonoperative casting or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Stable patterns with minimal step-off can heal successfully in a cylinder cast; surgery guarantees anatomical reduction but has high rates of hardware irritation and infection.

title: Traumatic meniscus tear in young adult: repair vs partial meniscectomy
canonical_question: In a young adult with a symptomatic traumatic meniscus tear in the vascular zone, is repair or partial meniscectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Repair preserves long-term joint loading and prevents osteoarthritis but has higher early failure and restricted rehab; meniscectomy offers rapid recovery but accelerates degeneration.

title: Mason Type II radial head fracture: nonoperative vs ORIF
canonical_question: In an adult with an isolated displaced Mason Type II radial head fracture without mechanical block, is nonoperative management or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Fractures without mechanical block typically do well nonoperatively; surgery addresses displacement but risks hardware prominence, stiffness, and nerve injury.

title: Thoracolumbar burst fracture (no neurological deficit): orthosis vs surgical stabilization
canonical_question: In an adult with a stable thoracolumbar burst fracture and no neurological deficit, is brace/orthosis immobilization or surgical stabilization preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Multiple RCTs show equivalent long-term functional and pain scores between brace treatment and surgery, but surgical management remains common due to faster mobilization.

title: Displaced intra-articular distal radius fracture (young adult): cast vs ORIF
canonical_question: In an active young adult with a displaced intra-articular distal radius fracture, is closed reduction and casting or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: ORIF restores intra-articular congruency to prevent long-term arthritis, but adds perioperative complications; casting avoids surgery but carries a higher risk of displacement.

title: Ligamentous Lisfranc injury: ORIF vs primary arthrodesis
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute purely ligamentous Lisfranc joint injury, is ORIF or primary arthrodesis preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Primary arthrodesis reduces the high rate of secondary salvage fusion and provides predictable stability, but sacrifices midfoot joint motion compared to ORIF.

title: Moderate carpal tunnel syndrome: surgical release vs splinting and injection
canonical_question: In an adult with moderate symptomatic carpal tunnel syndrome, is surgical release or splinting with corticosteroid injection preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Conservative therapy provides good temporary relief, but surgical release provides superior, long-term resolution of symptoms and nerve compression.

title: Acute Jones fracture (5th metatarsal): casting vs percutaneous screw fixation
canonical_question: In an active adult with an acute Jones fracture (5th metatarsal zone II), is nonoperative non-weightbearing casting or percutaneous screw fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgery results in faster union and quicker return to activity, particularly in athletes, but carries risk of hardware pain; casting is non-invasive but has higher nonunion rates.

title: Displaced isolated greater tuberosity fracture: nonoperative vs surgery
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced (>5mm) isolated greater tuberosity fracture, is nonoperative management or surgical fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Displacement can cause subacromial impingement, arguing for surgical fixation, yet nonoperative management with close observation often yields acceptable functional outcomes.

title: Chronic lateral ankle instability: functional rehabilitation vs Brostrom repair
canonical_question: In an adult with chronic symptomatic lateral ankle instability, is progressive functional rehabilitation or surgical Brostrom-Gould repair preferred?
equipoise_rationale: High-quality rehabilitation stabilizes many ankles dynamically; surgical repair is highly effective for persistent mechanical instability but risks nerve injury and stiffness.

title: Displaced Neer 2-part proximal humerus fracture: nonoperative vs surgical fixation
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced Neer 2-part proximal humerus fracture, is nonoperative management or surgical fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgical fixation restores anatomy but adds complications (screws cutout, avascular necrosis); nonoperative care accepts deformity but avoids operative risks.

title: Symptomatic gluteus medius tear: endoscopic/open repair vs physical therapy
canonical_question: In an adult with a chronic symptomatic gluteus medius or minimus tear, is structured physical therapy or surgical repair preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Repair provides good clinical improvement in tendon integrity and pain relief, but physical therapy remains a highly effective, lower-risk first-line option.

title: Chronic lateral epicondylitis: debridement vs structured conservative therapy
canonical_question: In an adult with chronic, recalcitrant lateral epicondylitis, is surgical debridement or structured conservative therapy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Debridement (Nirschl procedure) can resolve pain in refractory cases, but long-term outcomes often show little difference from natural history and persistent physical therapy.

title: Schatzker Type II tibial plateau fracture: nonoperative vs ORIF
canonical_question: In an adult with a Schatzker Type II tibial plateau fracture and <5mm of articular depression, is nonoperative non-weightbearing or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Minimal depression allows successful nonoperative management; surgery restores articular surface alignment but carries wound healing and infection risks.

title: Adhesive capsulitis: corticosteroid injection vs hydrodilatation
canonical_question: In an adult with primary adhesive capsulitis, is intra-articular corticosteroid injection or hydrodilatation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Both interventions provide rapid, temporary pain relief and range of motion improvement, but trials show conflicting evidence regarding the superior efficacy of hydrodilatation over standard injection.

title: Femoral shaft fracture: retrograde vs antegrade intramedullary nailing
canonical_question: In an adult with a femoral shaft fracture, is retrograde or antegrade intramedullary nailing preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Both techniques achieve high union rates; antegrade nailing risks hip pain and heterotopic ossification, while retrograde nailing risks knee pain and intra-articular irritation.

title: Hallux rigidus (stage III): cheilectomy vs primary arthrodesis
canonical_question: In an adult with Stage III hallux rigidus, is joint-preserving cheilectomy or primary arthrodesis preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Cheilectomy preserves motion but carries high rates of persistent pain and conversion to fusion; arthrodesis provides definitive pain relief but permanently eliminates joint motion.

title: Cervical disc herniation: ACDF vs artificial disc replacement
canonical_question: In an adult with single-level cervical disc herniation requiring surgery, is ACDF or artificial disc replacement (ADR) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: ADR preserves motion and may reduce adjacent segment disease, but long-term registry data are still accumulating compared to the gold-standard ACDF.

title: Scaphoid nonunion: vascularized vs non-vascularized bone graft
canonical_question: In an adult with a scaphoid proximal pole nonunion, is vascularized or non-vascularized bone grafting preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Vascularized grafts theoretically enhance healing in avascular segments, but clinical trials show variable benefit over traditional non-vascularized grafting techniques.

title: Advanced hip osteoarthritis: direct anterior vs posterior approach total hip arthroplasty
canonical_question: In an adult undergoing total hip arthroplasty for hip osteoarthritis, is the direct anterior or posterior approach preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Direct anterior approach may reduce early dislocation rates and speed early recovery, but posterior approach offers excellent visualization, lower fracture rates, and similar mid-term outcomes.

title: Total knee arthroplasty: cruciate-retaining vs posterior-stabilized
canonical_question: In an adult undergoing total knee arthroplasty, is a cruciate-retaining (CR) or posterior-stabilized (PS) design preferred?
equipoise_rationale: CR preserves natural kinematics and bone stock, while PS provides predictable stability and easier balancing; long-term functional and implant survival outcomes are equivalent.

title: Patellofemoral instability: isolated MPFL reconstruction vs MPFL with tibial tubercle osteotomy
canonical_question: In a patient with recurrent patellar dislocation and borderline TT-TG distance, is isolated MPFL reconstruction or combined MPFL with tibial tubercle osteotomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Combined surgery directly corrects bony malalignment but is more invasive; isolated soft-tissue reconstruction is less invasive but may under-address biomechanical drivers.

title: Cervical disc disease: ACDF vs cervical artificial disc replacement
canonical_question: In an adult with symptomatic single-level cervical disc disease, is ACDF or cervical artificial disc replacement (ADR) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: ADR preserves segmental cervical motion and potentially reduces adjacent segment disease, but long-term cohort survivorship data are still accumulating compared to standard ACDF.

title: Undisplaced femoral neck fracture (elderly): internal fixation vs hemiarthroplasty
canonical_question: In an active elderly patient with an undisplaced femoral neck fracture, is parallel screw internal fixation or hemiarthroplasty preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Internal fixation preserves native anatomy but has high rates of late displacement and avascular necrosis; primary hemiarthroplasty allows immediate stable weightbearing but is more invasive.

title: Developmental dysplasia of the hip: closed vs open reduction
canonical_question: In an infant with developmental dysplasia of the hip failing bracing, is closed reduction or open surgical reduction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Closed reduction under anesthesia is less invasive but has higher failure and osteonecrosis rates in select patterns; open reduction provides definitive clearance but carries surgical morbidity.

title: Pediatric femur fracture (age 2-6): elastic stable intramedullary nails vs spica cast
canonical_question: In a young child (age 2-6) with a displaced femoral shaft fracture, is percutaneous elastic stable intramedullary nailing (ESIN) or spica casting preferred?
equipoise_rationale: ESIN allows rapid mobilization and social convenience but carries anesthetic and hardware removal risks; spica casting avoids hardware surgery but imposes profound caregiving challenges.

title: Carpal tunnel syndrome: endoscopic vs open surgical release
canonical_question: In an adult with severe carpal tunnel syndrome, is endoscopic or open surgical release preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Endoscopic release offers faster early recovery and less scar tenderness, but carries a slightly higher risk of transient nerve injury and incomplete transverse ligament release compared to open.

title: Intertrochanteric hip fracture: cephalomedullary nail vs sliding hip screw
canonical_question: In an adult with a stable intertrochanteric hip fracture, is cephalomedullary nailing or sliding hip screw (SHS) fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Nailing offers theoretical biomechanical advantages but is more costly and has a risk of femoral split; sliding hip screws have a long track record of safety in stable patterns.

title: Proximal humerus fracture: surgical plating vs primary reverse shoulder arthroplasty
canonical_question: In an active elderly patient with a displaced 3- or 4-part proximal humerus fracture, is ORIF or primary reverse shoulder arthroplasty (RSA) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: ORIF preserves native anatomy but carries high risks of osteonecrosis and screw cutout; primary RSA provides predictable early function but limits long-term revision options.

title: Tibial plateau fracture: ORIF vs staged external fixation and spanning
canonical_question: In an adult with a high-energy bicondylar tibial plateau fracture, is primary ORIF or staged external fixation followed by delayed reconstruction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Primary ORIF allows anatomical reduction but carries high wound complication rates in swollen tissue; staged external fixation protects soft tissues but delays anatomical reconstruction.

title: Rotator cuff repair: single-row vs double-row suture anchor fixation
canonical_question: When surgical repair of a medium-to-large rotator cuff tear is indicated, is a single-row or double-row suture anchor technique preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Double-row repair provides superior biomechanical footprint coverage and lower re-tear rates, but single-row is faster, cheaper, and has equivalent clinical patient-reported outcomes.

title: Severe hallux valgus: Lapidus arthrodesis vs Scarf osteotomy
canonical_question: In an adult with severe hallux valgus and first ray hypermobility, is a Lapidus tarsometatarsal arthrodesis or Scarf metatarsal osteotomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Lapidus provides powerful multi-planar deformity correction and addresses hypermobility but requires prolonged non-weightbearing; Scarf allows faster mobilization but has higher recurrence in hypermobile rays.

title: Lumbar spinal stenosis: decompression alone vs decompression with interspinous spacer
canonical_question: In an adult with moderate single-level lumbar spinal stenosis, is decompression alone or decompression with insertion of an interspinous spacer preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Spacer placement preserves flexion and may limit mechanical destabilization, but increases cost, implant failure, and reoperation rates compared to gold-standard decompression alone.

title: Adult spinal deformity: fusion terminating at L5 vs pelvic fixation to the sacrum
canonical_question: In an adult undergoing long instrumented posterior spinal fusion for deformity, is terminating the construct at L5 or extending to the sacrum/pelvis preferred?
option_b_label: Extension of fusion to the sacrum
equipoise_rationale: Terminating at L5 preserves lumbosacral motion and reduces surgical time, but carries high rates of subsequent L5-S1 breakdown; extending to the pelvis protects L5-S1 but adds massive surgical morbidity.

title: Recurrent anterior shoulder instability: arthroscopic Bankart repair vs Latarjet procedure
canonical_question: In a patient with recurrent anterior shoulder instability and minimal bone loss, is arthroscopic soft-tissue Bankart repair or an open Latarjet coracoid transfer preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Latarjet dramatically reduces the recurrence rate, especially in high-risk patients, but is technically demanding with serious nerve and joint-overclosure risks compared to arthroscopic repair.

title: Slipping rib syndrome: surgical excision vs costal cartilage stabilization
canonical_question: In a patient with symptomatic slipping rib syndrome, is excision of the hypermobile costal cartilage segment or surgical plating/stabilization preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Segmental excision is simple and long-established but can lead to localized chest wall flaccidity; reconstruction/stabilization preserves chest wall integrity but adds hardware risks.

title: Pediatric femoral shaft fracture (age 5-10): spica casting vs elastic stable intramedullary nails
canonical_question: In a child aged 5-10 with a displaced femoral shaft fracture, is closed reduction and spica casting or percutaneous elastic stable intramedullary nailing preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Nails permit rapid return to school and peer integration but require secondary removal; casting avoids invasive surgery but requires prolonged, difficult home care.

title: Distal tibia pilon fracture: immediate ORIF vs staged external fixation followed by delayed ORIF
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute high-energy distal tibia pilon fracture, is immediate ORIF or staged spanning external fixation with delayed ORIF preferred?
option_b_label: Spanning external fixation with delayed ORIF
equipoise_rationale: Immediate ORIF secures anatomical reconstruction early but is associated with catastrophic wound breakdown and infection; staged care protects the soft tissue envelope but prolongs hospital stay.

title: Knee osteoarthritis: platelet-rich plasma (PRP) vs hyaluronic acid (HA) joint injection
canonical_question: In an adult with mild-to-moderate symptomatic knee osteoarthritis, is intra-articular PRP or hyaluronic acid injection preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Clinical trials show variable, highly contested efficacy; some meta-analyses suggest PRP provides superior anti-inflammatory symptom relief, but standard of care remains highly variable.

title: Displaced patellar fracture: tension-band wiring vs low-profile plate fixation
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced patellar fracture requiring surgery, is traditional tension-band wiring or low-profile plate fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Tension-band wiring is highly established but has a hardware removal rate of up to 40% due to prominent wires; plate fixation offers superior multi-planar stability but increases cost.

title: Femoroacetabular impingement: labral repair vs labral debridement during arthroscopy
canonical_question: During hip arthroscopy for FAI, is labral repair or labral debridement preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Repair preserves the suction-seal of the hip and slows cartilage degeneration, but is technically demanding and costly; debridement is fast and simple but may accelerate arthritis.

title: Lumbar disc herniation: endoscopic vs open microdiscectomy
canonical_question: In an adult with symptomatic lumbar disc herniation, is endoscopic discectomy or standard open microdiscectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Endoscopic approaches minimize paraspinal muscle dissection and shorten hospital recovery, but have a steep learning curve and similar long-term outcomes to open microdiscectomy.

title: Cervical radiculopathy: posterior foraminotomy vs anterior cervical discectomy and fusion
canonical_question: In an adult with single-level posterolateral cervical radiculopathy, is posterior foraminotomy or ACDF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Posterior foraminotomy avoids fusion and preserves motion but carries higher postoperative muscle pain; ACDF is highly reliable for nerve clearance but sacrifices segmental motion.

title: Proximal hamstring tendon avulsion: nonoperative rehabilitation vs surgical repair
canonical_question: In an active adult with a complete 2-tendon proximal hamstring avulsion, is nonoperative rehabilitation or surgical repair preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgical repair preserves peak power and reduces hamstring fatigue but carries sciatic nerve and wound risks; nonoperative care is non-invasive but may leave a residual strength deficit.

title: Anterior cervical fusion: structural allograft bone vs autogenous iliac crest bone graft
canonical_question: During ACDF, is structural allograft bone with plating or autogenous iliac crest bone graft (ICBG) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Autograft (ICBG) provides the highest fusion rate but is associated with severe, chronic donor-site pain; allograft avoids harvest morbidity but has a slightly higher nonunion rate.

title: Total knee arthroplasty: cemented vs cementless component fixation
canonical_question: In an active patient undergoing total knee arthroplasty, is cemented or cementless implant fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Cemented TKA is the historic gold standard with reliable, long-term survival; cementless TKA utilizes biological ingrowth to preserve bone but has higher component costs and rare early loosening.

title: Distal femur fracture: retrograde intramedullary nail vs lateral locking plate
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced extra-articular distal femur fracture, is retrograde distal femoral nailing or lateral locking plate fixation preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Retrograde nailing is a load-sharing, minimally invasive technique but can cause knee joint irritation; lateral plating allows anatomically precise fixation but requires extensive exposure.

title: Total hip arthroplasty: dual-mobility bearing vs standard single-bearing cup
canonical_question: In an older adult undergoing total hip arthroplasty with high risk of instability, is a dual-mobility bearing or a standard single-bearing cup preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Dual-mobility designs dramatically reduce postoperative dislocation rates, but introduce an additional theoretical interface for wear and potential intra-prosthetic dislocation.

title: Clavicle midshaft nonunion: plate fixation with bone graft vs plate fixation alone
canonical_question: In an adult with a symptomatic midshaft clavicle nonunion, is open reduction and plate fixation with bone grafting or plate fixation alone preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Bone grafting provides essential osteoinductive factors to stimulate healing in a nonunion, but plate fixation alone with rigid compression is often sufficient and avoids donor site morbidity.

title: Pediatric lateral condyle humerus fracture: nonoperative casting (displacement <3mm) vs ORIF
canonical_question: In a child with a displaced lateral condyle humerus fracture of 2-3mm, is nonoperative casting or surgical ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Displacements over 2mm have traditionally mandated ORIF to avoid nonunion and cubitus valgus; emerging evidence suggests stable patterns up to 3mm can be safely casted under close radiographic monitoring.

title: Closed humeral shaft fracture with acute radial nerve palsy: immediate exploration vs observation
canonical_question: In an adult with a closed humeral shaft fracture and acute primary radial nerve palsy, is immediate surgical exploration of the nerve or observation for spontaneous recovery preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Over 90% of closed primary radial nerve palsies recover spontaneously within 3-4 months, making initial observation standard; immediate exploration is preferred by some for definitive assessment.

title: Subacromial shoulder pain: physical therapy vs corticosteroid injection
canonical_question: In an adult with subacromial pain syndrome (impingement), is structured physical therapy or corticosteroid joint injection preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Corticosteroid injections provide rapid, transient pain relief but do not address mechanical deficits; physical therapy offers a durable, long-term functional cure but requires patient compliance.

title: Preventing knee osteoarthritis progression: structured dietary weight loss vs standard lifestyle advice
canonical_question: In an overweight or obese woman over the age of 50 with a high risk of knee osteoarthritis, is an intensive, structured dietary weight loss and exercise program or standard lifestyle advice preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Structured dietary weight loss combined with exercise (as shown in the TOPS trial) significantly unloads the joint, reduces systemic inflammatory markers, and prevents osteoarthritis progression.

title: Reduction of blood loss in major orthopedic surgery: prophylactic intravenous Tranexamic Acid vs standard care
canonical_question: In an adult undergoing major reconstructive orthopedic or trauma surgery, is prophylactic intravenous Tranexamic Acid or standard care (no TXA) preferred to reduce blood loss?
equipoise_rationale: Evidenced by CRASH-2 and clinical guidelines, prophylactic intravenous TXA significantly reduces blood loss and transfusion requirements without elevating thromboembolic risks.

title: Displaced distal radius fracture: volar locking plate vs percutaneous Kirschner wiring
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced extra-articular distal radius fracture, is open reduction and volar locking plate fixation or closed reduction and percutaneous Kirschner wiring preferred?
equipoise_rationale: The DRAFFT trial demonstrated that both volar locking plates and K-wires result in equivalent functional outcomes at one year; volar plating is far more expensive but allows faster mobilization.

title: Iliotibial band syndrome: structured physical therapy vs surgical release
canonical_question: In an adult with painful iliotibial band syndrome, is structured physical therapy or surgical release of the IT band preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Structured physical therapy, focusing on hip abductor strengthening and local biomechanical corrections, is highly effective; surgical release is not indicated initially.

title: Uncomplicated patellar tendinopathy: eccentric exercise rehabilitation vs surgical tenotomy
canonical_question: In an adult with uncomplicated patellar tendinopathy (jumper's knee), is eccentric exercise rehabilitation or surgical tendon debridement/tenotomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Progressive tendon loading via eccentric exercise is the gold standard, demonstrating excellent long-term clinical outcomes; invasive surgery is not indicated first-line.

title: Uncomplicated sternoclavicular joint sprain: sling immobilization vs reconstruction
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute, uncomplicated (non-displaced) sternoclavicular joint sprain, is sling immobilization or surgical reconstruction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Uncomplicated sternoclavicular sprains heal reliably with temporary sling immobilization and symptomatic care; surgical reconstruction is not indicated.

title: Chronic sacroiliac joint pain: structured physical therapy vs surgical fusion
canonical_question: In an adult with chronic, uncomplicated sacroiliac joint pain, is structured physical therapy or surgical SI joint fusion preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Guidelines and clinical trials support structured core, pelvic floor, and hip stabilization exercises; surgical fusion is not indicated for uncomplicated joint pain.

title: Moderate knee osteoarthritis (no locking): arthroscopic debridement vs conservative care
canonical_question: In an adult with moderate knee osteoarthritis and no mechanical lock, is arthroscopic debridement or structured conservative care preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Multiple randomized trials show no benefit of arthroscopic debridement over structured physical therapy and medical management for osteoarthritic knees without locking.

title: Uncomplicated acute coccydynia: nonoperative pelvic unloading vs coccygectomy
canonical_question: In an adult with acute, uncomplicated coccydynia, is nonoperative pelvic unloading (ergonomic cushions, NSAIDs) or surgical coccygectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Conservative management resolves over 90% of cases; surgical coccygectomy is highly invasive with high wound contamination risks and is not indicated.

title: Greater trochanteric pain syndrome: physical rehabilitation vs surgical bursectomy
canonical_question: In an adult with uncomplicated greater trochanteric pain syndrome, is structured physical rehabilitation or surgical bursectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Physical therapy targeting hip abductor strength and tissue conditioning is standard; surgical bursectomy offers no additional value in primary cases.

title: Undisplaced scapular spine fracture: sling immobilization vs surgical ORIF
canonical_question: In an adult with an isolated undisplaced fracture of the scapular spine, is sling immobilization or surgical ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Undisplaced scapular fractures heal reliably with excellent functional outcomes using short-term sling immobilization; surgery is unnecessary and contraindicated.

title: Medial tibial stress syndrome: physical rehabilitation vs surgical fasciotomy
canonical_question: In an athlete with medial tibial stress syndrome (shin splints), is structured physical rehabilitation or surgical posterior fasciotomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Progressive load management, orthotics, and physical rehabilitation are standard; surgical fasciotomy is highly invasive with unproven clinical efficacy.

title: Morton's neuroma: orthotic offloading vs surgical neurectomy
canonical_question: In an adult with symptomatic, uncomplicated Morton's neuroma, is orthotic offloading/corticosteroid injection or surgical neurectomy preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Conservative measures (wide toe-box shoes, metatarsal pads, localized injections) successfully manage the majority of cases; surgical excision is reserved for late failures.

title: Garden Type I femoral neck fracture: nonoperative management vs percutaneous pinning
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute Garden Type I (valgus impacted/incomplete) femoral neck fracture, is nonoperative management or percutaneous pinning preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Percutaneous pinning is standard to prevent catastrophic secondary displacement and avascular necrosis, which occur in up to 15% of nonoperatively managed cases.

title: Vancouver Type B1 periprosthetic femur fracture: plate ORIF vs stem revision
canonical_question: In a patient with a Vancouver Type B1 periprosthetic femur fracture (stable stem), is locking plate ORIF or stem revision arthroplasty preferred?
equipoise_rationale: In a B1 fracture where the prosthetic stem remains solidly fixed to bone, locking plate ORIF is standard; revising a stable stem adds unnecessary surgical bone loss.

title: Vertically unstable pelvic ring injury: skeletal traction vs surgical stabilization
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute vertically unstable pelvic ring fracture, is skeletal traction alone or surgical stabilization (ORIF/exfix) preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Surgical stabilization is mandatory to restore pelvic height, decrease retroperitoneal volume, permit early patient mobilization, and reduce the high mortality of recumbency.

title: Acute posterior sternoclavicular dislocation: nonoperative observation vs closed/open reduction
canonical_question: In an adult with an acute posterior sternoclavicular dislocation, is nonoperative observation or urgent reduction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Posterior dislocations place the trachea, esophagus, and great mediastinal vessels at immediate risk; urgent reduction (often with cardiothoracic backup) is mandatory.

title: Open calcaneus fracture: nonoperative wound management vs emergent surgical debridement
canonical_question: In an adult with an open calcaneal fracture, is nonoperative wound care or emergent surgical debridement and stabilization preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Open intra-articular calcaneus fractures require emergent operative debridement, antibiotic irrigation, and soft-tissue stabilization to prevent limb-threatening osteomyelitis.

title: Atlantoaxial subluxation with myelopathy: cervical immobilization vs posterior surgical fusion
canonical_question: In an adult with atlantoaxial subluxation and signs of progressive cervical myelopathy, is cervical collar immobilization or posterior surgical fusion preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Progressive myelopathy driven by atlantoaxial instability requires posterior surgical stabilization and fusion to prevent permanent quadriplegia or respiratory arrest.

title: Displaced talar neck fracture: closed cast immobilization vs open reduction internal fixation
canonical_question: In an adult with a displaced talar neck fracture (Hawkins Type II-IV), is closed cast immobilization or ORIF preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Displaced talus fractures carry a near 100% risk of avascular necrosis and malunion if left unreduced; urgent anatomical ORIF is the absolute standard of care.

title: Chronic long-bone osteomyelitis: long-term antibiotics alone vs surgical debridement with antibiotics
canonical_question: In an adult with chronic, localized long-bone osteomyelitis, is long-term antibiotics alone or surgical debridement with antibiotics preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Chronic osteomyelitis involves necrotic bone (sequestrum) that cannot be penetrated by systemic antibiotics; surgical debridement of dead bone is mandatory for cure.

title: Acute knee dislocation: knee brace immobilization vs surgical multi-ligament reconstruction
canonical_question: In an active adult after an acute knee dislocation with multi-ligament instability, is simple knee brace immobilization or surgical reconstruction preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Knee dislocations result in profound joint instability and high rates of arthrofibrosis; surgical stabilization/reconstruction is required to restore basic joint kinematics.

title: Progressive adolescent idiopathic scoliosis (Cobb angle 55): bracing vs posterior spinal fusion
canonical_question: In an adolescent with progressive idiopathic scoliosis and a Cobb angle of 55 degrees, is bracing or posterior spinal fusion preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Curves exceeding 50 degrees at skeletal maturity continue to progress throughout adulthood; posterior spinal fusion is the standard of care.

title: Open pediatric femur fracture: skeletal traction alone vs emergent surgical debridement and fixation
canonical_question: In a child with an open femoral shaft fracture, is skeletal traction alone or emergent surgical debridement with stabilization preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Open long-bone fractures require immediate surgical debridement and stabilization to prevent bone infection and nonunion, regardless of patient age.

title: Distal radius fracture with acute median nerve compression: splinting/reduction vs carpal tunnel decompression
canonical_question: In an adult with a distal radius fracture and progressive, acute median nerve paresthesias despite reduction, is splinting or urgent carpal tunnel decompression preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Unresolved acute median nerve compression following fracture reduction causes irreversible axonal loss; urgent surgical release of the transverse carpal ligament is mandatory.

title: Traumatic knee arthrotomy (joint penetration): nonoperative observation vs surgical joint washout
canonical_question: In an adult with a traumatic wound demonstrating joint penetration (arthrotomy), is nonoperative wound care or urgent surgical joint washout preferred?
equipoise_rationale: Traumatic penetration introduces bacteria directly into the sterile joint space; urgent surgical debridement and irrigation are required to prevent septic arthritis.
